ACADEMIC MEDICAL CENTER  
OPERATIVE REPORT  

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Lumbar spinal stenosis at L4–5  
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same  

PROCEDURE: L4–5 decompressive laminectomy  

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE:  
The patient was brought to the operating room and placed prone on a radiolucent table. After standard preparation and draping, a midline incision was made over L4–5. Subperiosteal dissection exposed the laminae. A laminectomy was performed using a high-speed drill and Kerrison rongeurs. The ligamentum flavum was removed, resulting in adequate decompression of the thecal sac and nerve roots. Hemostasis was achieved. The wound was irrigated and closed in layers.

No complications were encountered.

REGIONAL SPINE CENTER  
OPERATIVE REPORT  

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Lumbar disc herniation at L5–S1  
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same  

PROCEDURE: Right L5–S1 microdiscectomy  

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE:  
After exposure of the lamina, a partial laminotomy was performed. During removal of adherent ligamentum flavum, a small tear in the dura was noted. Cerebrospinal fluid egress was observed. The defect was repaired primarily using a 6-0 Prolene suture and reinforced with a dural sealant. No further leakage was seen on Valsalva maneuver. The discectomy was completed without additional issues.

The patient tolerated the procedure well.

UNIVERSITY HOSPITAL  
OPERATIVE REPORT  

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Degenerative spondylolisthesis at L4–5  
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same  

PROCEDURE: L4–5 posterior lumbar interbody fusion  

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE:  
Pedicle screws were placed at L4 and L5 bilaterally. During placement of the right L5 screw, a decrease in motor evoked potentials was detected. The screw was repositioned, after which signals returned to baseline. No further changes occurred. Interbody cages were placed and rods secured. Final fluoroscopy confirmed appropriate hardware position.

Estimated blood loss was minimal.

CITY MEDICAL CENTER  
OPERATIVE REPORT  

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Thoracic spinal stenosis at T10–11  
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same  

PROCEDURE: T10–11 decompressive laminectomy  

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE:  
Following exposure, the laminae were removed using a drill. Brisk epidural venous bleeding was encountered and controlled with bipolar cautery and hemostatic agents. After achieving hemostasis, decompression was completed without further difficulty. The wound was irrigated and closed in layers.

No additional complications occurred.
